label: Stripe Checkout
summary: Hosted Stripe Checkout for one-time and subscription payments. Adds a server route that creates checkout sessions and a client button that redirects to Stripe's hosted page. When STRIPE_SECRET_KEY is missing the route returns 503 'payments-not-configured' and the button degrades to a calm IntegrationConfigNotice + disabled state instead of a raw error. Webhook handling is intentionally out of scope — add separately when subscriptions need lifecycle events.

envVars.purpose: Server-side Stripe API authentication. Find it in the Stripe dashboard under Developers > API keys. Tagged 'feature-runtime' (like resend-contact-form): the checkout-session route instantiates the Stripe client lazily AFTER the env guard, so a missing key degrades to 503 'payments-not-configured' + IntegrationConfigNotice instead of blocking the F3 build or crashing the deploy.

Client-side Stripe.js initialization. Safe to expose; the secret key is never sent to the browser. Tagged 'warn-only' because the publishable key is in the harmless placeholder set and a missing real value only disables the checkout button gracefully.
